item.title: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: NewCore argues the next challenge in enterprise security will be managing AI agents, not people.

item.title: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: In April, for the first time ever, an Earth observation satellite found what it was looking for, all on its own.

item.title: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: What makes this combustible: at the very moment that tens of thousands of workers are being shown the door, a small cohort of AI insiders is becoming wealthy on a scale that's hard to comprehend.

item.title: Orbio raises $21 million to automate hiring and onboarding for frontline workers
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Orbio announces $21 Million Series A in round led by Dawn Capital.

item.title: Startup CEO Charlie Javice is reportedly angling for a Trump pardon
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: JPMorgan can't be pleased by any of this.

item.title: UK may ban social media for children under 16
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The U.K. seems to be following Australia's lead in banning a wide swath of social media for teens.

item.title: As AI companies race to go public, who else is along for the ride?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Startups are trying to "ride that SpaceX IPO wave."

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: SpaceX rockets past Tesla
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: The new Sonos Play has become my go-to desk and kitchen speaker
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The new Sonos Play can act as a portable speaker inside and outside your home.

item.title: As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Tech leaders debate whether the Anthropic episode is a wake-up call for India’s AI ambitions.

item.title: Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2B Manus deal after Beijing’s demand
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Meta starts dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition after Beijing ordered the deal reversed.

item.title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Once again, AI proves to be an unreliable source of information about AI.

item.title: Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before government crackdown
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy may have been the source of security concerns that led Anthropic to cut off worldwide access to two models on Friday.

item.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: It's not clear which states are involved, but they're asking about everything from OpenAI's ad policies to its handling of health data.

item.title: This thin under-pillow speaker helped me fall asleep without earbuds
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: I’ve struggled with insomnia since I was very young. Like many chronic overthinkers, I tend to fall asleep best when my mind is occupied by something else, such as podcasts, YouTube compilations, or my personal favorite: rain sounds. But earbuds can be uncomfortable, and playing audio out loud isn’t exactly considerate when I’m staying at […]

item.title: The FBI built its own replica small town to simulate real-world cyberattacks
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Hidden inside a building in Alabama, the FBI has created its own small town as a dedicated cyber training ground for simulating cyberattacks.

item.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Andrew Yang made a list of everything Americans overpay for — housing, food, wireless — and thinks the next startup gold rush is giving that money back.

item.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.

item.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Russell Brandom
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

item.title: Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: A new report suggests the unit, which employs 6,500 people, is on the verge of revolt.
